summary: High‑energy 1993 Boston show captured on a sweet soundboard recording, highlighted by a smoking China Cat/Rider and an often‑cited “So Many Roads” performance.
review: The show is praised for being in peak ’93 form with standout moments like the blazing China Cat → Rider sequence and a frequently‑called‑best‑ever rendition of So Many Roads. The SBD source is generally clear and balanced, though a few listeners note lightness in the mix and occasional crackle or mic hiccups. Overall the performance outweighs minor recording quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (10/14) give 5‑star ratings and extol both the music and the SBD sound, with only a handful of lower scores pointing to specific song or recording issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: SBD is described as sweet and clear; some note it is a bit light for certain ears and mention occasional crackling in set 1 and a mic mishap at the start of a song.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set shines with high‑energy transitions and a powerful drum/space segment; overall the concert flows strongly, reflecting the band’s peak ’93 form.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for great vibe and strong vocal delivery (headlight comment), solid guitar tone, though one reviewer notes his guitar and voice slipped on the final verse of So Many Roads.
band_member_comments.Drums: Multiple reviewers highlight the drums and space as exemplary of the 1990s Dead, describing swirling, circular sound that moves the body.
